## What's Changed

- Replaced web UI login internals with a true password-only server auth flow.
- Removed `jwt_secret` generation and persistence from server configuration.
- Server login now stores one bcrypt password hash in config and uses a signed `TT_SESSION` cookie derived from that hash.
- Removed username/email handling from setup, login, and reset-password web UI API calls.
- Existing configs with no saved password now correctly show password setup instead of a broken login prompt.
- Existing configs with a saved password now report setup as complete to both new and cached web UI clients.
- Login now appears only when a server password exists; no password means the web UI opens normally.
- Removed the separate login-required toggle from the web UI so authentication has one rule: set a password to enable login, remove the password to disable login.
- Queue, health, and log streaming endpoints now validate the same password session cookie as the rest of the web UI.

## Upgrade

Pull the latest container and restart:

```bash
docker pull ghcr.io/tatertotterson/tater-tube-server:latest
docker restart tater-tube-server
```

After updating, set the server password from the web UI. Existing `jwt_secret` lines are ignored by this version and will disappear the next time the server saves config.
